diff:
+## Fixture workspace (seed-db)
+
+`boot.sh` runs `bun scripts/qa/seed-db.ts` against the fresh profile before launch: the
+app's own drizzle migrations, then a deterministic workspace — one project pointing at the
+checkout under test, an empty chat, and a seeded chat with a short user/assistant exchange.
+Rules when extending `fixtures.ts` (they keep the vision judge honest):
+
+  must render pixel-identically.
+  a failing run there means the fixtures went stale against a migration, fix them before
+  they surface as a blank QA screen.
+  Electron-ABI and crashes plain node/bun scripts.
+
+  chat" panel over chat surfaces. It is deterministic (identical in base and head boots), so
+  judge comparisons stay valid — but chat-input/message-thread QA intents are blocked until
note: real frink commit: feat(qa): deterministic fixture workspace seeded into the QA profile (#71)

+## Pull requests (PR-at-leaf)
+
+A `dependsOn` edge means "needs the code of" — a chain of dependent stages is ONE line of work, and each stage's branch already contains every ancestor stage's commits. PRs follow from that:
+
+
+

diff:
+slug: flow-editor-runs-tab-ia
+created: 2026-07-10
+
+# flow-editor-runs-tab-ia
+
+## Target · 2026-07-10 — FlowEditor has ONE always-visible tab pair Editor Runs
+
+**Context:** User feedback: 'is flows part of monitor or vice versa', undiscoverable restart, no way to see multiple batches per flow. Old IA: Monitor tab existed only when a batch existed (state-dependent UI) plus three overlapping run surfaces (Monitor tab, Runs side panel, its Batch sub-tab). Research across Archon/n8n/windmill/trigger.dev/Airflow/Dagster/Temporal/Prefect: dominant pattern is monitoring co-located with the editor as an always-visible surface, run list -> big run detail, retry actions on the thing you are looking at.
+**Ruling:** FlowEditor has ONE always-visible tab pair Editor|Runs; 'Monitor' dies as a noun. Runs tab is n8n-style master/detail: 300px rail (batch groups + run rows, selectable, Active chip on currentBatchId) + main pane = selected batch's stage DAG (BatchMonitor + action toolbar) OR selected run's detail (RunDetailPane; newest run auto-selected so the pane is never empty). Selection is UI-local view state and never mutates currentBatchId (the persisted run target); run actions render on exactly ONE surface at a time (header FlowRunButton hides whenever the monitor pane's toolbar is visible). Progressive disclosure: a flow with zero batches never shows the word 'batch' (batches only mint via briefing, so the vocabulary is earned). Carry on / Restart verbs unchanged from flow-run-restart-recovery; batch actions shared via use-batch-run-actions so header + toolbar derive identical state.
+**Consequences:**
+**Vision-fit:** USP is ease of use: junior users see runs, never batch jargon they did not opt into; pros keep full batch orchestration one click deep. Monitoring stays a facet of a flow (tool, not platform), matching the chat->automation on-ramp.
+**Scope:** src/renderer/features/flows/FlowEditor/**, src/renderer/hooks/use-batch-run-actions.ts, src/renderer/lib/utils/default-selected-batch.ts
+**Source:** manual
+/**
note: real frink commit: feat(flows): Runs tab IA redesign + batch carry-on (one monitoring surface) (#75
